message:
fix(guard,share,storage): glob separator semantics, shell quoting, and Python compat tests

Guard (guard/lib.rs):
- Fix glob matching to use literal_separator(true) via GlobBuilder,
  matching the Python glob_to_regex behavior where * matches [^/]* and
  ** matches .*. Previously the bare Glob::new call allowed * to cross
  directory separators, causing false-positive path conflicts.
- Remove unused globset::Glob import (now uses GlobBuilder directly).
- Fix install_guard to use the actual project parameter name instead of
  underscore-prefixed _project.

Share (share/planner.rs):
- Add shell quoting helpers (quote_path, quote_str) that apply POSIX
  single-quote escaping for paths and strings containing shell
  metacharacters. Prevents command injection and breakage with paths
  containing spaces, quotes, or special characters.
- Apply quoting to all generated shell commands in GitHub Pages,
  Cloudflare Pages, Netlify, and S3 deployment plans: mkdir, cp, touch,
  git push, wrangler deploy, netlify deploy, aws s3 sync/cp, and
  cloudfront invalidation commands.

Storage (storage/lib.rs):
- Add comprehensive Python archive compatibility test suite verifying
  Rust can read Python-format archives:
  - project.json with human_key field
  - agent profile.json with/without optional fields
  - message bundles with JSON frontmatter (---json / ---)
  - file reservations (both "path" and "path_pattern" keys)
  - Python timestamp formats (RFC3339 with offset, Z suffix, naive, space separator)
  - thread digest markdown files
  - SHA1-named reservation artifacts
  - empty project edge case
  - archive consistency checks against Python-format messages

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
